Explore County-Level Turnout

All 437 counties across Illinois, Indiana, Ohio, Wisconsin, and Michigan — plotted three ways. Toggle between actual turnout, what the model predicts given each county’s demographics, and the gap between them.

How to Use Use the layer selector (top right of the map) to switch views. Click any county to see a profile card with its actual and predicted turnout, model residual, and key demographic characteristics. Counties selected as case studies are outlined in black — hover to identify them.

Counties outlined in gray are the six case studies. Data: ACS 2020 5-Year Estimates; State Election Commissions (IL, IN, OH, WI, MI); CVAP Special Tabulation. Predictions from Model 4 (12 predictors + state fixed effects).

  • Background → Descriptive findings: state choropleths and demographic gap analysis
  • Methodology → Full model specification, results, and residual distribution
  • Case Studies → Qualitative analysis of six selected counties
  • Synthesis → Cross-case patterns and implications

Interactive map built with R and Leaflet for R. Choropleth boundaries from the U.S. Census Bureau (TIGER/Line, 2023). Full analysis source on GitHub.
